nodes.x: My son, forget not my law; but let thine heart keep my commandments:
Keep my commandments, and live; and my law as the apple of thine eye.
¶ My son, keep thy father’s commandment, and forsake not the law of thy mother:
AND now, my children, I command you: Fear the Lord your God with your whole heart, and walk in simplicity according to all His law.

Every good and wholesome law we should obey strictly, and do it with a
good and honest-heart. 11:134.
Hear thou, my son, and be wise, and guide thine heart in the way.
For length of days, and long life, and peace, shall they add to thee.

And their lives shall be increased in peace,
          And the years of their joy shall be multiplied,
          In eternal gladness and peace,
          All the days of their life.
That it may be well with thee, and thou mayest live long on the earth.

[paragraph continues] 7. "And now I give thanks unto Thee, my God, because Thou hast caused me to see this day: behold, I am one hundred three score and fifteen years, an old man and full of days, 1 and all my days have been unto me peace. 8. The sword of the adversary 2 hath not overcome me in all that Thou hast given me and my children all the days of my life until this day. 9. My God, may Thy…

rest upon thy servants who shall endeavour to build upon the same — may that spirit of unanimity and peace that pervades our bosoms this morning, rest upon those who shall labour Upon this building; may it also rest upon their wives and children, and extend itself throughout the length and breadth of this territory, and the whole world, that the honest in heart may rejoice, and thy Saints be…

Thou shalt keep therefore his statutes, and his commandments, which I command thee this day, that it may go well with thee, and with thy children after thee, and that thou mayest prolong thy days upon the earth, which the Lord thy God giveth thee, for ever.
Let not mercy and truth forsake thee: bind them about thy neck; write them upon the table of thine heart:
For thy mercy is great unto the heavens, and thy truth unto the clouds.
For thy mercy is great above the heavens: and thy truth reacheth unto the clouds.
The Lord will perfect that which concerneth me: thy mercy, O Lord, endureth for ever: forsake not the works of thine own hands.
Justice and judgment are the habitation of thy throne: mercy and truth shall go before thy face.
Let thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
So shalt thou find favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.
Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.
Then shalt thou understand righteousness, and judgment, and equity; yea, every good path.
Trust in the Lord, and do good; so shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.
For whoso findeth me findeth life, and shall obtain favour of the Lord.
Therefore remember, O man, for all thy doings thou shalt be brought into judgment.
¶ Trust in the Lord with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.

Trust in the Lord and Lean Not By Bonnie H. Cordon Second Counselor in the Primary General Presidency We can center our lives on the Savior by coming to know Him, and He will direct our paths. While I was traveling in Asia, a dear sister approached me. She wrapped her arms around me and asked, “Do you really believe that this gospel is true?” Dear sister, I know it is true. I trust in the Lord.…
So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, and apply thine heart to understanding;
Grant thee according to thine own heart, and fulfil all thy counsel.
¶ When wisdom entereth into thine heart, and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul;
Delight thyself also in the Lord; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.

Therefore, verily I acknowledge him and will bless him, and also thee, inasmuch as thou art faithful in counsel, in the office which I have appointed unto you, in prayer always, vocally and in thy heart, in public and in private, also in thy ministry in proclaiming the gospel in the land of the living, and among thy brethren.
And he shall bring forth thy righteousness as the light, and thy judgment as the noonday.

3. Therefore, verily I acknowledge him and will bless him, and
    also thee, inasmuch as thou art faithful in counsel, in the office
    which I have appointed unto you, in prayer, always, vocally and
    in thy heart, in public and in private, also in thy ministry in
    proclaiming the Gospel in the land of the living, and among thy
    brethren;

"Confident that he will honor this high trust as well as ardently fulfill his commission as a messenger of peace and salvation as one of the Lord's noble men, I can fervently say, may the Lord clear his way before him and bless him and bless those that obey his teachings wherever there are ears to hear and hearts to feel.
¶ Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the Lord, and depart from evil.
Cease from anger, and forsake wrath: fret not thyself in any wise to do evil.
And unto man he said, Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.
And the afflicted people thou wilt save: but thine eyes are upon the haughty, that thou mayest bring them down.

O that cunning plan of the evil one! O the vainness, and the frailties, and the foolishness of men! When they are learned they think they are wise, and they hearken not unto the counsel of God, for they set it aside, supposing they know of themselves, wherefore, their wisdom is foolishness and it profiteth them not. And they shall perish.

Thine own wickedness shall correct thee, and thy backslidings shall reprove thee: know therefore and see that it is an evil thing and bitter, that thou hast forsaken the Lord thy God, and that my fear is not in thee, saith the Lord God of hosts.
It shall be health to thy navel, and marrow to thy bones.
And all saints who remember to keep and do these sayings, walking in obedience to the commandments, shall receive health in their navel and marrow to their bones;
My soul shall be satisfied as with marrow and fatness; and my mouth shall praise thee with joyful lips:
And the Lord thy God will circumcise thine heart, and the heart of thy seed, to love the Lord thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul, that thou mayest live.
Honour the Lord with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase:
The firstfruit also of thy corn, of thy wine, and of thine oil, and the first of the fleece of thy sheep, shalt thou give him.

Thus saith the Lord God; Because thy filthiness was poured out, and thy nakedness discovered through thy whoredoms with thy lovers, and with all the idols of thy abominations, and by the blood of thy children, which thou didst give unto them;
¶ Trust in the Lord with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
So shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine.
Come ye, say they, I will fetch wine, and we will fill ourselves with strong drink; and to morrow shall be as this day, and much more abundant.
Now therefore the wheat, and the barley, the oil, and the wine, which my lord hath spoken of, let him send unto his servants:

Then shall he give the rain of thy seed, that thou shalt sow the ground withal; and bread of the increase of the earth, and it shall be fat and plenteous: in that day shall thy cattle feed in large pastures.

And gladness is taken away, and joy out of the plentiful field; and in the vineyards there shall be no singing, neither shall there be shouting: the treaders shall tread out no wine in their presses; I have made their vintage shouting to cease.

And, behold, I will give to thy servants, the hewers that cut timber, twenty thousand measures of beaten wheat, and twenty thousand measures of barley, and twenty thousand baths of wine, and twenty thousand baths of oil.
¶ My son, despise not the chastening of the Lord; neither be weary of his correction:
If ye endure chastening, God dealeth with you as with sons; for what son is he whom the father chasteneth not?
And ye have forgotten the exhortation which speaketh unto you as unto children, My son, despise not thou the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when thou art rebuked of him:
Thou shalt also consider in thine heart, that, as a man chasteneth his son, so the Lord thy God chasteneth thee.
For whom the Lord loveth he correcteth; even as a father the son in whom he delighteth.
For whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom he receiveth.
For he received from God the Father honour and glory, when there came such a voice to him from the excellent glory, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
And because he dwelleth in flesh he shall be called the Son of God, and having subjected the flesh to the will of the Father, being the Father and the Son—
Correct thy son, and he shall give thee rest; yea, he shall give delight unto thy soul.
For it became him, for whom are all things, and by whom are all things, in bringing many sons unto glory, to make the captain of their salvation perfect through sufferings.
¶ Happy is the man that findeth wisdom, and the man that getteth understanding.
Happy is the man that feareth alway: but he that hardeneth his heart shall fall into mischief.
Happy are thy men, and happy are these thy servants, which stand continually before thee, and hear thy wisdom.
He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.
Happy are thy men, happy are these thy servants, which stand continually before thee, and that hear thy wisdom.
For the merchandise of it is better than the merchandise of silver, and the gain thereof than fine gold.
It cannot be gotten for gold, neither shall silver be weighed for the price thereof.
My fruit is better than gold, yea, than fine gold; and my revenue than choice silver.
Surely there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold where they fine it.
She is more precious than rubies: and all the things thou canst desire are not to be compared unto her.
¶ Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.
For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it.
There is gold, and a multitude of rubies: but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.

And it shall be, if thou have no delight in her, then thou shalt let her go whither she will; but thou shalt not sell her at all for money, thou shalt not make merchandise of her, because thou hast humbled her.
Length of days is in her right hand; and in her left hand riches and honour.
For an odious woman when she is married; and an handmaid that is heir to her mistress.

She sought riches and obtained them; yet in the day of prosperity she
remembered the poor, for she dealt out her substance to the needy, with
a liberal hand, to the end of her days, and died the object of their
affection. As she was beloved in life, so she was bewailed in death.
The right hand of the Lord is exalted: the right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly.
Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.

For thus saith the Lord, Behold, I will extend peace to her like a river, and the glory of the Gentiles like a flowing stream: then shall ye suck, ye shall be borne upon her sides, and be dandled upon her knees.
That she may settle down in peace inasmuch as she is faithful, and not be idle in her days from thenceforth.
She is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her: and happy is every one that retaineth her.

"She is one of the people that are naturally rich," her mother added,
"in having for her property a sunny, healthy content, and a happy,
humble disposition. We shall all be glad to see her when she comes for
her visit by-and-by. A spirit like hers brings its own welcome wherever
it goes."
Her love, her friendship, enmity with God; Her promises, a lie; her smile, a harlot’s;
The Lord by wisdom hath founded the earth; by understanding hath he established the heavens.
He hath made the earth by his power, he hath established the world by his wisdom, and hath stretched out the heaven by his understanding.
He hath made the earth by his power, he hath established the world by his wisdom, and hath stretched out the heavens by his discretion.

For thus saith the Lord that created the heavens; God himself that formed the earth and made it; he hath established it, he created it not in vain, he formed it to be inhabited: I am the Lord; and there is none else.
Behold, the Lord hath created the earth that it should be inhabited; and he hath created his children that they should possess it.
The Lord hath prepared his throne in the heavens; and his kingdom ruleth over all.
By his knowledge the depths are broken up, and the clouds drop down the dew.
He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; and the cloud is not rent under them.
Dost thou know the balancings of the clouds, the wondrous works of him which is perfect in knowledge?
Which the clouds do drop and distil upon man abundantly.
Thick clouds are a covering to him, that he seeth not; and he walketh in the circuit of heaven.
As the cloud is consumed and vanisheth away: so he that goeth down to the grave shall come up no more.
¶ My son, let not them depart from thine eyes: keep sound wisdom and discretion:
Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.

Only take heed to thyself, and keep thy soul diligently, lest thou forget the things which thine eyes have seen, and lest they depart from thy heart all the days of thy life: but teach them thy sons, and thy sons’ sons;
¶ My son, attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings.
Thy sons and thy daughters shall be given unto another people, and thine eyes shall look, and fail with longing for them all the day long: and there shall be no might in thine hand.
¶ My son, despise not the chastening of the Lord; neither be weary of his correction:
So shall they be life unto thy soul, and grace to thy neck.
For they shall be an ornament of grace unto thy head, and chains about thy neck.
And shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.
Behold, O Lord, their souls are precious, and many of them are our brethren; therefore, give unto us, O Lord, power and wisdom that we may bring these, our brethren, again unto thee.

Verily, verily I say unto you, blessed are such, for they shall obtain; for I, the Lord, show mercy unto all the meek, and upon all whomsoever I will, that I may be justified when I shall bring them unto judgment.
In thy name shall they rejoice all the day: and in thy righteousness shall they be exalted.
Then shalt thou walk in thy way safely, and thy foot shall not stumble.
When thou goest, thy steps shall not be straitened; and when thou runnest, thou shalt not stumble.
Therefore thou shalt keep the commandments of the Lord thy God, to walk in his ways, and to fear him.
My son, walk not thou in the way with them; refrain thy foot from their path:

And shake off the dust of thy feet against those who receive thee not, not in their presence, lest thou provoke them, but in secret; and wash thy feet, as a testimony against them in the day of judgment.
They shall bear thee up in their hands, lest thou dash thy foot against a stone.
When thou liest down, thou shalt not be afraid: yea, thou shalt lie down, and thy sleep shall be sweet.
Also thou shalt lie down, and none shall make thee afraid; yea, many shall make suit unto thee.
I will both lay me down in peace, and sleep: for thou, Lord, only makest me dwell in safety.
When thou goest, it shall lead thee; when thou sleepest, it shall keep thee; and when thou awakest, it shall talk with thee.
And thy life shall hang in doubt before thee; and thou shalt fear day and night, and shalt have none assurance of thy life:
And it shall be, when he lieth down, that thou shalt mark the place where he shall lie, and thou shalt go in, and uncover his feet, and lay thee down; and he will tell thee what thou shalt do.
Be not afraid of sudden fear, neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it cometh.
When your fear cometh as desolation, and your destruction cometh as a whirlwind; when distress and anguish cometh upon you.
The fear of the wicked, it shall come upon him: but the desire of the righteous shall be granted.
The transgression of the wicked saith within my heart, that there is no fear of God before his eyes.
The fear of the Lord prolongeth days: but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.
For the Lord shall be thy confidence, and shall keep thy foot from being taken.
Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee, knowing that thou wilt also do more than I say.
Then shalt thou walk in thy way safely, and thy foot shall not stumble.
¶ Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to do it.
For there is verily a disannulling of the commandment going before for the weakness and unprofitableness thereof.
And now, behold, we are in thine hand: as it seemeth good and right unto thee to do unto us, do.
Thine hand shall be lifted up upon thine adversaries, and all thine enemies shall be cut off.
Say not unto thy neighbour, Go, and come again, and to morrow I will give; when thou hast it by thee.
If thou borrowest of thy neighbor, thou shalt restore that which thou hast borrowed; and if thou canst not repay then go straightway and tell thy neighbor, lest he condemn thee.
I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.
If thou at all take thy neighbour’s raiment to pledge, thou shalt deliver it unto him by that the sun goeth down:
And it shall be given thee from the time thou shalt go, until the time thou shalt return, what thou shalt do.
Devise not evil against thy neighbour, seeing he dwelleth securely by thee.
Thou shalt not speak evil of thy neighbor, nor do him any harm.
But I say unto you, That ye resist not evil: but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.
The soul of the wicked desireth evil: his neighbour findeth no favour in his eyes.
But I say unto you, that ye shall not resist evil, but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also;
¶ Strive not with a man without cause, if he have done thee no harm.
For he doth not afflict willingly nor grieve the children of men.
Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters.
There are those who have sought occasion against him without cause;
¶ Envy thou not the oppressor, and choose none of his ways.
¶ He is a merchant, the balances of deceit are in his hand: he loveth to oppress.
I have done judgment and justice: leave me not to mine oppressors.
Ye shall not therefore oppress one another; but thou shalt fear thy God: for I am the Lord your God.
And oppress not the widow, nor the fatherless, the stranger, nor the poor; and let none of you imagine evil against his brother in your heart.
For the froward is abomination to the Lord: but his secret is with the righteous.
He that justifieth the wicked, and he that condemneth the just, even they both are abomination to the Lord.
They that are of a froward heart are abomination to the Lord: but such as are upright in their way are his delight.
The way of the wicked is an abomination unto the Lord: but he loveth him that followeth after righteousness.
¶ He said also unto me, Turn thee yet again, and thou shalt see greater abominations that they do.
For all that do these things are an abomination unto the Lord: and because of these abominations the Lord thy God doth drive them out from before thee.
¶ The curse of the Lord is in the house of the wicked: but he blesseth the habitation of the just.
The righteous man wisely considereth the house of the wicked: but God overthroweth the wicked for their wickedness.
And behold, a curse shall come upon the land, saith the Lord of Hosts, because of the people’s sake who are upon the land, yea, because of their wickedness and their abominations.
He that saith unto the wicked, Thou art righteous; him shall the people curse, nations shall abhor him:
And he leadeth away the righteous into precious lands, and the wicked he destroyeth, and curseth the land unto them for their sakes.
There shall no evil happen to the just: but the wicked shall be filled with mischief.
Surely he scorneth the scorners: but he giveth grace unto the lowly.
A scorner loveth not one that reproveth him: neither will he go unto the wise.
Reprove not a scorner, lest he hate thee: rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee.
For assuredly as the Lord liveth they shall see that the terrible one is brought to naught, and the scorner is consumed, and all that watch for iniquity are cut off;
The thought of foolishness is sin: and the scorner is an abomination to men.
The wise shall inherit glory: but shame shall be the promotion of fools.
The crown of the wise is their riches: but the foolishness of fools is folly.
I said unto the fools, Deal not foolishly: and to the wicked, Lift not up the horn:
For ye suffer fools gladly, seeing ye yourselves are wise.
Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools,
